romania.title: O fetiță de 2 ani a murit în ambulanță, după ce i s-a făcut rău, în Constanța. Poliția a deschis o anchetă
romania.summary: Poliţiştii au deschis o anchetă după ce o fată de un an şi nouă luni din judeţul Constanţa a murit, după ce a început să se simtă rău, iar mama a decis să o transporte cu maşina la medicul de familie. Pe drum, starea fetiţei s-a agravat, iar femeia a sunat la 112 şi pe drum s-a întâlnit cu o ambulanţă. Echipajul medical a început manevrele de resuscitare, dar fără rezultat.

romania.summary: DevTalks România ajunge, în 2026, la cea de-a 13-a ediție, confirmând fără echivoc statutul său de cel mai așteptat eveniment de technologie din România și din Europa Centrală și de… Articolul Cea mai mare conferință de tehnologie din România și CEE are loc pe 3–4 iunie la Romexpo, B1 apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: 😂 Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in
romania.summary: Veşti bune pentru pasagerii companiilor aeriene low-cost! Wizz Air a anunţat că va păstra neschimbate preţurile biletelor pentru majoritatea destinaţiilor, impunându-le călătorilor o singură condiţie: să aducă la check-in un bidon, butoiaş, găleată sau damigeană cu 10 litri de kerosen. „La întoarcere, pasagerii îşi vor putea recupera bidonul de la acelaşi birou de check-in, iar […] The post Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: O nouă fabrică digitală Siemens în România: cu emisii zero de carbon și cu procese bazate pe transferul de date
romania.summary: O nouă fabrică Siemens, digitalizată și bazată pe inteligență artificială, va fi construită la Sibiu până în 2028, după obținerea avizelor necesare, pe baza unei decizii strategice de investiție pentru… Articolul O nouă fabrică digitală Siemens în România: cu emisii zero de carbon și cu procese bazate pe transferul de date apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

Imaginea… Articolul World Press Photo 2026: Imaginea anului surprinde drama separării unei familii de migranți în SUA. Expoziția internațională ajunge și anul acesta la București, în luna mai apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Vladimir Plahotniuc mai are o armă în lupta cu justiţia! Condamnat la 19 ani pentru crimă organizată, escrocherie şi spălare de bani, oligarhul moldovean şi-a amintit că are şi paşaport românesc şi a invocat dreptul de a o suna pe Lia, dobândit odată cu cetăţenia română. Potrivit procedurii, Lia poate să răspundă sau nu, dar […] The post Auzind că i-au dat 19 ani, Plahotniuc şi-a invocat dreptul de cetăţean român să o sune pe Lia appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 Bolojan a salvat economia! România va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca
romania.summary: Prezenţa premierului Bolojan în studiul Pro TV nu a fost întâmplătoare! Mai mult, acesta a ieşit victorios din sediul televiziunii de pe Pache Protopopescu după ce a negociat un credit foarte avantajos pentru România. Pe scurt, ţara noastră va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca, bani care urmează să fie folosiţi pentru […] The post Bolojan a salvat economia! România va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
romania.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

romania.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
romania.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.title: Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești”
romania.summary: United Way România lucrează acolo unde nevoia este cea mai mare, acolo unde sprijinul nu este doar util, ci vital. Fără acest ajutor, mulți copii ar rămâne în urmă, riscând să se piardă în dificultățile vieții. De aceea, apreciem sprijinul pe termen lung pe care Raiffeisen Bank îl oferă programului „Învață să reușești”. Printre copiii […] Articolul Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești” apare prima dată în United Way Romania .

romania.title: Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate
romania.summary: De ce vorbim despre bullying Bullyingul nu mai este de mult o problemă „minoră” sau o etapă inevitabilă a copilăriei.... Articolul Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Descrierea postului: Părintele social crează un mediu de viaţă, îngrijire şi educaţie, caracterizat prin afecţiune şi stabilitate şi adecvat dezvoltării personalităţii fiecărui copil (în plan fizic, psihic, emoţional, social şi... The post Părinte Social appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

romania.source: Hope and Homes for Children Romania
romania.title: „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj
romania.summary: Hope and Homes for Children, alături de Distribuție Oltenia, parte din Evryo, a derulat campania „Grijă pentru mine”, un proiect dedicat sănătății emoționale aadolescentelor din medii vulnerabile. Inițiativa a ajuns la peste 250 de fete din județul Gorj, inclusiv tinere din sistemul de protecție și din comunități rurale, oferindu-le sprijin pentru a-și înțelege emoțiile, a-șicrește... Articolul „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj apare prima dată în Hope and Homes for Children .

romania.title: De la un proiect Teach for Romania la o inițiativă europeană: cum elevii din Reghin construiesc „Leadership HUB”
romania.summary: O idee pornită din dorința de a dezvolta competențele de leadership ale elevilor s-a transformat, în doar câțiva ani, într-o inițiativă educațională care conectează școli din mai multe țări europene. Povestea proiectului „Leadership HUB” începe cu un proiect finanțat de Teach for Romania și continuă astăzi prin colaborări internaționale în cadrul platformei eTwinning și al […]

medical.title: Move smarter not harder: How less exercise is more
medical.summary: If you think you need to "go hard" at the gym to make your muscles stronger, think again. New research at ECU has revealed strenuous exercise and soreness is not needed to improve muscle size, strength, or performance.

medical.title: Severe strokes linked to 5 times higher dementia risk
medical.summary: As stroke severity increases, the risk of progressive cognitive decline and dementia substantially escalates, according to a national study led by Michigan Medicine researchers. People with the most severe strokes had five times higher odds of developing dementia and showed cognitive decline equivalent to being more than two years older at baseline compared with people who did not have a stroke. The results are published in JAMA Network Open.

medical.title: Voting is linked to living longer
medical.summary: A new study co-authored by Femida Handy of the School of Social Policy & Practice at the University of Pennsylvania finds that, among older adults, voting predicts a lower risk of mortality for up to 15 years.

medical.title: Dual-pathway blockade therapy offers hope for rare, hard-to-treat gynecological clear cell cancers
medical.summary: Before her cancer diagnosis in early 2017, 48-year-old Caitlin Delaney, who is based in Australia, had been struggling with a troubling mix of symptoms comprising extreme fatigue, chronic sinusitis, and appetite changes including a sudden aversion to foods such as chocolate and alcohol. Detailed tests and scans eventually revealed the cause—an advanced stage of clear cell ovarian cancer. For another patient, Michelle (not her real name), aged 49, the road to diagnosis came in late 2022 after months of persistent acid reflux, bloating, and ongoing indigestion, which similarly pointed to the same cancer—clear cell carcinoma of the ovary.

medical.title: Two-faced protein discovery may explain why leukemia drugs fail or succeed
medical.summary: A KAIST research team has identified the real reason why anticancer drugs kill cancer cells—targeted anticancer therapies do not simply block cancer proteins but rather shut down the "protein factories" inside the cells, forcing them to undergo self-destruction. Consequently, the "two-faced protein" that plays a key role in this process is gaining attention as a breakthrough for treating patients with drug resistance.

medical.title: Finding the best dietary supplements for cycling performance—and recovery
medical.summary: Focusing on the rigors of elite cycling, Flinders University experts have put performance-enhancing and other dietary supplements under the microscope, rating some more highly than others. From carbs, beetroot juice and the latest sports gels, the new research highlights how a range of dietary supplements may help improve cycling performance, working to support the body's energy systems and reducing fatigue during exercise.

medical.title: TAVR Google searches surge over 300% in a decade: Corresponds with expanded adoption of transcatheter approaches
medical.summary: New research found that public interest in Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) increased substantially over the last decade, with Google search volume rising by 340% from 2015 to 2025, while searches for SAVR (Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement) declined by 42%. These trends parallel a shift in practice, as clinicians doubled TAVR use between 2015 and 2021.

medical.title: Using a new online mental health tool just once boosts UK teenagers' hope and emotional well-being
medical.summary: Research finds that one-time use of a free online tool can positively support young people's mental health and well-being. Developed by psychologists and health researchers at the University of Bath, Project Action Brings Change (ABC) UK is a free digital support tool—known as a digital single‑session intervention (SSI)—that provides short, accessible and evidence-based psychological support and well-being activities.

medical.title: 3D DNA switch in brown fat could reshape obesity and diabetes treatment
medical.summary: Most fat stores energy; the body's brown fat does the opposite. Unlike the white fat that accumulates just under our skin, brown fat burns calories and glucose to generate heat. Formally known as brown adipose tissue, it is a specialized metabolic tissue whose importance in adults has only come into focus in recent years. Brown fat—stored in small, distinct pockets deep within the body—plays an outsized role in body-weight regulation and blood-sugar control, making it an attractive target for metabolic therapies.

medical.title: Eating more fruits and vegetables tied to unexpected lung cancer risk
medical.summary: A surprising new study suggests that eating a very healthy diet—packed with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains—might be linked to a higher risk of lung cancer in younger non-smokers. Researchers found that patients under 50 diagnosed with lung cancer often had better-than-average diets, raising the possibility that pesticide exposure from conventionally grown produce could be a hidden culprit.

science.title: Is your cat or dog overweight? Why simply feeding less doesn't always help
science.summary: Overweight and obesity are among the most common conditions veterinarians see in both dogs and cats.

science.title: Chernobyl at 40: The lies, the loss and why we can't let go
science.summary: Some historical events are so catastrophic they resist comprehension. And yet they compel us to try to understand them, again and again.

science.title: Quantum chips could scale faster with new spin-qubit readout that reduces sensors and wiring
science.summary: Quantum computers, devices that process information leveraging quantum mechanical effects, could tackle some tasks that are difficult or impossible to solve using classical computers. These systems represent data as qubits, units of information that can exist in multiple states at once, unlike the bits used by classical computers that represent data using binary values ("0" or "1").

science.title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
science.summary: On every crewed mission, NASA packs pouches of a potentially life-saving liquid in its cargo, known as IV (or intravenous) fluid. A simple mix of sodium chloride and purified water, it can treat up to 30% of medical conditions in flight, resolving things like dehydration, burns, and more. Crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit into […]

science.title: This flower's toxic traits hold clues for safer drugs
science.summary: The molecules of a highly toxic plant, known for its bell-shaped purple and pink flowers and found in some home gardens, have long been used to regulate human heart muscles.

science.title: Did NASA's Curiosity rover find signs of ancient life on Mars? An astrobiologist explains how we determine 'life'
science.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified seven new organic compounds on the planet Mars, according to new research published in Nature Communications.

science.title: Climate finance may lower conflict risk in 85 developing countries, analysis suggests
science.summary: Just as major global powers are retreating from climate finance commitments, a new empirical study provides, for the first time, evidence of a direct link between climate finance and a lower risk of resource-related conflict in developing countries.

science.title: Mysterious gas clouds near Milky Way's black hole now have a likely source
science.summary: New observations and simulations by a team of researchers led by MPE reveal that a massive binary star near our galaxy's center is responsible for creating a series of enigmatic gas clouds—compact gas clumps that help feed the supermassive black hole Sagittarius A*. The study is published in the journal Astronomy & Astrophysics.

science.title: We eat a lot of wheat. So how can we grow more in a changing climate?
science.summary: Whether it's tucking into some toast, dumplings or a bowl of fresh pasta, humans love eating wheat.

science.title: Research shows community help essential for native bats
science.summary: Community help is no longer just nice to have in the world of bat conservation, it is essential to large-scale bat monitoring and the protection of threatened and understudied species, according to new research from Murdoch University's School of Environmental and Conservation Sciences. The study reveals that collaboration between universities, not-for-profits, government agencies and community members is the key to ensuring data collection is accurate, and that it flows into policy and practice.

science.title: Black hole jets measured for first time and rival the power of 10,000 suns
science.summary: Scientists have captured stunning new insights into one of the universe’s most powerful phenomena—black hole jets—by using a planet-sized network of radio telescopes. Focusing on Cygnus X-1, one of the first known black holes, they measured jets blasting out with the energy of 10,000 Suns and moving at half the speed of light. By watching these jets get pushed and bent by the fierce stellar winds of a nearby supergiant star, researchers could calculate their true power for the first time.

science.title: What caffeine does to ants could change pest control
science.summary: Caffeine doesn’t just perk up humans—it can sharpen ants’ minds too. Invasive Argentine ants given caffeinated sugar learned to find food much more efficiently, taking straighter paths and reducing travel time by up to 38%. They weren’t faster, just more focused, indicating improved learning. This unexpected effect could make pest control baits far more effective.

science.title: Light makes plants stronger but also holds them back
science.summary: Light doesn’t just help plants grow—it may also quietly hold them back. Researchers have uncovered a surprising mechanism where light strengthens the “glue” between a plant’s outer skin and its inner tissues. This tighter bond, driven by a compound called p-coumaric acid, reinforces cell walls but also restricts how much the plant can expand. The discovery reveals a hidden balancing act: light both fuels growth and subtly puts the brakes on it.

environment.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
environment.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

environment.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

environment.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
environment.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

environment.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
environment.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

environment.title: Lake Coatepeque
environment.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

environment.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
environment.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

environment.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
environment.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

environment.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
environment.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

Imaginea… Articolul World Press Photo 2026: Imaginea anului surprinde drama separării unei familii de migranți în SUA. Expoziția internațională ajunge și anul acesta la București, în luna mai apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: 😂 Bolojan a salvat economia! România va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca
mix_items.summary: Prezenţa premierului Bolojan în studiul Pro TV nu a fost întâmplătoare! Mai mult, acesta a ieşit victorios din sediul televiziunii de pe Pache Protopopescu după ce a negociat un credit foarte avantajos pentru România. Pe scurt, ţara noastră va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca, bani care urmează să fie folosiţi pentru […] The post Bolojan a salvat economia! România va împrumuta 4 miliarde de euro de la Andreea Esca appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.title: O fetiță de 2 ani a murit în ambulanță, după ce i s-a făcut rău, în Constanța. Poliția a deschis o anchetă
mix_items.summary: Poliţiştii au deschis o anchetă după ce o fată de un an şi nouă luni din judeţul Constanţa a murit, după ce a început să se simtă rău, iar mama a decis să o transporte cu maşina la medicul de familie. Pe drum, starea fetiţei s-a agravat, iar femeia a sunat la 112 şi pe drum s-a întâlnit cu o ambulanţă. Echipajul medical a început manevrele de resuscitare, dar fără rezultat.

mix_items.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
mix_items.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas
mix_items.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale
mix_items.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Psiholog
mix_items.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
mix_items.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
mix_items.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Move smarter not harder: How less exercise is more
mix_items.summary: If you think you need to "go hard" at the gym to make your muscles stronger, think again. New research at ECU has revealed strenuous exercise and soreness is not needed to improve muscle size, strength, or performance.

mix_items.title: Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești”
mix_items.summary: United Way România lucrează acolo unde nevoia este cea mai mare, acolo unde sprijinul nu este doar util, ci vital. Fără acest ajutor, mulți copii ar rămâne în urmă, riscând să se piardă în dificultățile vieții. De aceea, apreciem sprijinul pe termen lung pe care Raiffeisen Bank îl oferă programului „Învață să reușești”. Printre copiii […] Articolul Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești” apare prima dată în United Way Romania .

mix_items.title: Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate
mix_items.summary: De ce vorbim despre bullying Bullyingul nu mai este de mult o problemă „minoră” sau o etapă inevitabilă a copilăriei.... Articolul Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Is your cat or dog overweight? Why simply feeding less doesn't always help
mix_items.summary: Overweight and obesity are among the most common conditions veterinarians see in both dogs and cats.

mix_items.title: Părinte Social
mix_items.summary: Descrierea postului: Părintele social crează un mediu de viaţă, îngrijire şi educaţie, caracterizat prin afecţiune şi stabilitate şi adecvat dezvoltării personalităţii fiecărui copil (în plan fizic, psihic, emoţional, social şi... The post Părinte Social appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.source: Hope and Homes for Children Romania
mix_items.title: „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj
mix_items.summary: Hope and Homes for Children, alături de Distribuție Oltenia, parte din Evryo, a derulat campania „Grijă pentru mine”, un proiect dedicat sănătății emoționale aadolescentelor din medii vulnerabile. Inițiativa a ajuns la peste 250 de fete din județul Gorj, inclusiv tinere din sistemul de protecție și din comunități rurale, oferindu-le sprijin pentru a-și înțelege emoțiile, a-șicrește... Articolul „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj apare prima dată în Hope and Homes for Children .

mix_items.title: Chernobyl at 40: The lies, the loss and why we can't let go
mix_items.summary: Some historical events are so catastrophic they resist comprehension. And yet they compel us to try to understand them, again and again.

mix_items.title: De la un proiect Teach for Romania la o inițiativă europeană: cum elevii din Reghin construiesc „Leadership HUB”
mix_items.summary: O idee pornită din dorința de a dezvolta competențele de leadership ale elevilor s-a transformat, în doar câțiva ani, într-o inițiativă educațională care conectează școli din mai multe țări europene. Povestea proiectului „Leadership HUB” începe cu un proiect finanțat de Teach for Romania și continuă astăzi prin colaborări internaționale în cadrul platformei eTwinning și al […]

mix_items.title: Quantum chips could scale faster with new spin-qubit readout that reduces sensors and wiring
mix_items.summary: Quantum computers, devices that process information leveraging quantum mechanical effects, could tackle some tasks that are difficult or impossible to solve using classical computers. These systems represent data as qubits, units of information that can exist in multiple states at once, unlike the bits used by classical computers that represent data using binary values ("0" or "1").

mix_items.title: Severe strokes linked to 5 times higher dementia risk
mix_items.summary: As stroke severity increases, the risk of progressive cognitive decline and dementia substantially escalates, according to a national study led by Michigan Medicine researchers. People with the most severe strokes had five times higher odds of developing dementia and showed cognitive decline equivalent to being more than two years older at baseline compared with people who did not have a stroke. The results are published in JAMA Network Open.

mix_items.title: Voting is linked to living longer
mix_items.summary: A new study co-authored by Femida Handy of the School of Social Policy & Practice at the University of Pennsylvania finds that, among older adults, voting predicts a lower risk of mortality for up to 15 years.

mix_items.title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
mix_items.summary: On every crewed mission, NASA packs pouches of a potentially life-saving liquid in its cargo, known as IV (or intravenous) fluid. A simple mix of sodium chloride and purified water, it can treat up to 30% of medical conditions in flight, resolving things like dehydration, burns, and more. Crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit into […]

mix_items.title: This flower's toxic traits hold clues for safer drugs
mix_items.summary: The molecules of a highly toxic plant, known for its bell-shaped purple and pink flowers and found in some home gardens, have long been used to regulate human heart muscles.

mix_items.title: Dual-pathway blockade therapy offers hope for rare, hard-to-treat gynecological clear cell cancers
mix_items.summary: Before her cancer diagnosis in early 2017, 48-year-old Caitlin Delaney, who is based in Australia, had been struggling with a troubling mix of symptoms comprising extreme fatigue, chronic sinusitis, and appetite changes including a sudden aversion to foods such as chocolate and alcohol. Detailed tests and scans eventually revealed the cause—an advanced stage of clear cell ovarian cancer. For another patient, Michelle (not her real name), aged 49, the road to diagnosis came in late 2022 after months of persistent acid reflux, bloating, and ongoing indigestion, which similarly pointed to the same cancer—clear cell carcinoma of the ovary.

mix_items.title: Did NASA's Curiosity rover find signs of ancient life on Mars? An astrobiologist explains how we determine 'life'
mix_items.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified seven new organic compounds on the planet Mars, according to new research published in Nature Communications.

mix_items.title: Two-faced protein discovery may explain why leukemia drugs fail or succeed
mix_items.summary: A KAIST research team has identified the real reason why anticancer drugs kill cancer cells—targeted anticancer therapies do not simply block cancer proteins but rather shut down the "protein factories" inside the cells, forcing them to undergo self-destruction. Consequently, the "two-faced protein" that plays a key role in this process is gaining attention as a breakthrough for treating patients with drug resistance.

mix_items.title: Climate finance may lower conflict risk in 85 developing countries, analysis suggests
mix_items.summary: Just as major global powers are retreating from climate finance commitments, a new empirical study provides, for the first time, evidence of a direct link between climate finance and a lower risk of resource-related conflict in developing countries.

mix_items.title: Finding the best dietary supplements for cycling performance—and recovery
mix_items.summary: Focusing on the rigors of elite cycling, Flinders University experts have put performance-enhancing and other dietary supplements under the microscope, rating some more highly than others. From carbs, beetroot juice and the latest sports gels, the new research highlights how a range of dietary supplements may help improve cycling performance, working to support the body's energy systems and reducing fatigue during exercise.

mix_items.title: Mysterious gas clouds near Milky Way's black hole now have a likely source
mix_items.summary: New observations and simulations by a team of researchers led by MPE reveal that a massive binary star near our galaxy's center is responsible for creating a series of enigmatic gas clouds—compact gas clumps that help feed the supermassive black hole Sagittarius A*. The study is published in the journal Astronomy & Astrophysics.

mix_items.title: TAVR Google searches surge over 300% in a decade: Corresponds with expanded adoption of transcatheter approaches
mix_items.summary: New research found that public interest in Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) increased substantially over the last decade, with Google search volume rising by 340% from 2015 to 2025, while searches for SAVR (Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement) declined by 42%. These trends parallel a shift in practice, as clinicians doubled TAVR use between 2015 and 2021.

mix_items.title: We eat a lot of wheat. So how can we grow more in a changing climate?
mix_items.summary: Whether it's tucking into some toast, dumplings or a bowl of fresh pasta, humans love eating wheat.

mix_items.title: Research shows community help essential for native bats
mix_items.summary: Community help is no longer just nice to have in the world of bat conservation, it is essential to large-scale bat monitoring and the protection of threatened and understudied species, according to new research from Murdoch University's School of Environmental and Conservation Sciences. The study reveals that collaboration between universities, not-for-profits, government agencies and community members is the key to ensuring data collection is accurate, and that it flows into policy and practice.

mix_items.title: Using a new online mental health tool just once boosts UK teenagers' hope and emotional well-being
mix_items.summary: Research finds that one-time use of a free online tool can positively support young people's mental health and well-being. Developed by psychologists and health researchers at the University of Bath, Project Action Brings Change (ABC) UK is a free digital support tool—known as a digital single‑session intervention (SSI)—that provides short, accessible and evidence-based psychological support and well-being activities.

mix_items.title: 3D DNA switch in brown fat could reshape obesity and diabetes treatment
mix_items.summary: Most fat stores energy; the body's brown fat does the opposite. Unlike the white fat that accumulates just under our skin, brown fat burns calories and glucose to generate heat. Formally known as brown adipose tissue, it is a specialized metabolic tissue whose importance in adults has only come into focus in recent years. Brown fat—stored in small, distinct pockets deep within the body—plays an outsized role in body-weight regulation and blood-sugar control, making it an attractive target for metabolic therapies.

mix_items.title: Black hole jets measured for first time and rival the power of 10,000 suns
mix_items.summary: Scientists have captured stunning new insights into one of the universe’s most powerful phenomena—black hole jets—by using a planet-sized network of radio telescopes. Focusing on Cygnus X-1, one of the first known black holes, they measured jets blasting out with the energy of 10,000 Suns and moving at half the speed of light. By watching these jets get pushed and bent by the fierce stellar winds of a nearby supergiant star, researchers could calculate their true power for the first time.

mix_items.title: What caffeine does to ants could change pest control
mix_items.summary: Caffeine doesn’t just perk up humans—it can sharpen ants’ minds too. Invasive Argentine ants given caffeinated sugar learned to find food much more efficiently, taking straighter paths and reducing travel time by up to 38%. They weren’t faster, just more focused, indicating improved learning. This unexpected effect could make pest control baits far more effective.

mix_items.title: Eating more fruits and vegetables tied to unexpected lung cancer risk
mix_items.summary: A surprising new study suggests that eating a very healthy diet—packed with fruits, vegetables, and whole grains—might be linked to a higher risk of lung cancer in younger non-smokers. Researchers found that patients under 50 diagnosed with lung cancer often had better-than-average diets, raising the possibility that pesticide exposure from conventionally grown produce could be a hidden culprit.

mix_items.title: Light makes plants stronger but also holds them back
mix_items.summary: Light doesn’t just help plants grow—it may also quietly hold them back. Researchers have uncovered a surprising mechanism where light strengthens the “glue” between a plant’s outer skin and its inner tissues. This tighter bond, driven by a compound called p-coumaric acid, reinforces cell walls but also restricts how much the plant can expand. The discovery reveals a hidden balancing act: light both fuels growth and subtly puts the brakes on it.

mix_items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
mix_items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

mix_items.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
mix_items.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

mix_items.title: Lake Coatepeque
mix_items.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

mix_items.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
mix_items.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
mix_items.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

mix_items.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
mix_items.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

joke_ro.text: Mi-am organizat biroul pe categorii: urgente, importante si maine.

satire_ro.title: Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in
